senses.definition: In Indian philosophy, the faculty of wisdom or insight that perceives the true nature of reality.
examples: The teacher spoke of prajna as the deep, awakening wisdom beyond ordinary knowledge.
Meditation aims to sharpen prajna and see beyond appearances.

senses.definition: Plural of prajna; multiple instances of deep wisdom or insight.
examples: The sutras describe several prajnas that arise in enlightened minds.
His prajnas enabled him to interpret complex teachings clearly.

senses.definition: A given name of Sanskrit origin meaning light or illumination; used in various Indian contexts.
examples: Prakash is a common male given name in South Asia.
In the festival, Prakash illuminated the temple with lamps.

senses.definition: Any of a set of ancient Indian languages that are precursors to modern Indo-Aryan languages; the term also refers to the family as a whole.
examples: Prakrits were used in many early Indian literary works.
Linguists study the relationship between Sanskrit and Prakrits.

senses.definition: In Indian philosophy, the natural or material world; the unconscious nature of the universe, contrasted with purusha (spirit).
examples: Prakriti refers to the changing material world in Sankhya philosophy.
Understanding prakriti helps explain the roots of suffering.

senses.definition: Relating to prakriti; produced by or characteristic of the natural world; not spiritual.
examples: The artist preferred prakritic colors that echoed the earth.
Prakritic processes shape the changing landscape.

senses.definition: To render or treat something as prakritic or in the form of a Prakrit language; to simplify into an indigenous form of speech.
examples: Linguists might prakritize a sentence to study its roots.
The author chose to prakritize the dialogue for a regional flavor.

senses.definition: A confections made of nuts (usually almonds or hazelnuts) coated in sugar candy.
examples: She bought a box of praline chocolates.
The praline filling was sweet and crunchy.

senses.definition: Plural of praline; several praline confections.
examples: She shared pralines with her friends.
The gift basket included assorted pralines.

senses.definition: A rapid, trilled musical ornament (a short trill) used in classical music, especially in German music terminology.
examples: The violinist executed a precise pralltriller as written in the score.
The term pralltriller appears in many baroque and classical compositions.

senses.definition: Plural of pralltriller; multiple short trills in music.
examples: The passages include several pralltrillers that require precision.
Her scores listed many pralltrillers throughout the piece.

senses.definition: A baby carriage; a small pushable vehicle for carrying a baby.
examples: She pushed the pram around the park.
The baby slept peacefully in the pram.

senses.definition: Unclear or rare usage; not widely attested in contemporary English.
examples: The term pramnian appears in a very old text.

senses.definition: plural of pram; baby carriages.
examples: The park was full of prams outside the café.
They bought two prams for twins.

senses.definition: In yoga and Indian philosophy, vital life force energy that sustains life.
examples: Breathing exercises circulate prana through the body.
The practice focuses on directing prana for better health.

senses.definition: Plural of prana; multiple life force energies.
examples: The text describes the movement of pranas within the body.
Different practices aim to balance pranas.

senses.definition: The sacred syllable Om (A-U-M) in Hinduism; considered the source of all sounds and the universal sound.
examples: Chanting pranava is believed to align breath with consciousness.
The practitioner repeated pranava to center the mind.

senses.definition: A controlled breathing technique in yoga to influence the flow of prana.
examples: She practices pranayama to calm her nerves before performances.
Different pranayama methods regulate inhalation, retention, and exhalation.

senses.definition: Plural of pranayama; multiple breathing techniques in yoga.
examples: The class learned several pranayamas for relaxation.
Advanced pranayamas require guidance from a teacher.

senses.definition: To move with lively, springy steps in a proud or showy way.
examples: The horse pranced around the arena.
She pranced onto the stage in a bright dress.

senses.definition: Past tense of prance; moved with lively steps.
examples: The pony pranced across the field.
She pranced away after the compliment.

senses.definition: Characterized by prancing; lively, showy in movement.
examples: The horse gave a pranceful display in the ring.

senses.definition: A horse or person that prances; someone who moves proudly.
examples: The horse became a crowd favorite as the proud prancer.
She played the role of the prancer in the school performance.

senses.definition: Plural of prancer; multiple prancers.
examples: The parade featured several prancers wearing bright costumes.

senses.definition: Third-person singular of prance; moves with a springy, showy step.
examples: The horse prances around the arena.
She prances with confidence when delivering tickets.
